titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/240

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=209 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-02 13:02:56.71368+00 | translation moa=208 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-02 13:02:56.723639+00

p.cl:
Willelmus dei gratia Rex Scott’. Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiis . Vicecomitibus . ⁊ omnibus
suis probis hominibus tam futuris quam presentibus salutem.

Sciatis me concessisse ⁊ hac mea carta con
firmasse ecclesie Sancte Crucis que est iuxta edenesburch ⁊ fratribus ibidem deo seruientibus . Ecclesiam
de treuernent . quam eis Malcolmus Rex frater meus ad indumenta eorum dedit in perpetuam
elemosinam . ⁊ carta sua confirmauit . cum omnibus pertinentiis ⁊ libertatibus que predicte ecclesie
iuste pertinent . terris uidelicet ⁊ aquis . pascuis ⁊ pratis . bosco ⁊ plano . ita liberam ⁊ quietam ut
aliqua elemosina in tota terra mea liberius ⁊ quietius possidetur . ⁊ sicut Walerannus capel
lanus Dauid Regis eam melius unquam ⁊ liberius tenuit.

his testibus . Nicholao cancellario .
Ricardo capellano Regis . Waltero dapifero . Ricardo de moreuila . Dauid
Olifiard.
William by the grace of God king of Scots to the Bishops, Abbots, Earls, Barons, Justices, Sheriffs, and all his worthy men both future as well as present, Greeting:

Know that I have granted and by this my charter made firm to the church of Holyrood that is near Edinburgh and to the brethren serving God there, the Church of Tranent, which King Malcolm my brother gave to them for their clothing in perpetual alms, and made firm by his charter, with all pertinents and liberties which rightfully belong to the said church; by lands, that is, and waters, pastures and meadows, wood and plain, as free and quiet as other alms in the whole of my land are freely and quietly possessed, and as Waleran, King David’s chaplain, ever well and freely held it.
With these witnesses, Nicholas the chancellor, Richard the king’s chaplain, Walter the steward, Richard de Morville, David Oliphant.
